zh.lifeSummary: 當Prince Gwilym Ap Gwrwared於大約1140年出生在英國威爾斯布洛可爾時，他的父親Gwnvared ap Cuhelin26歲，母親Gwrangen Comer23歲。他於大約1163年與Annes verch Seisyll of Coed Rhath在英國威爾斯結婚。他們至少是6個兒子的父母。他去世於1195年之後。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Prince Gwilym Ap Gwrwared于大约1140年出生在英国威尔斯布洛可爾时，他的父亲Gwnvared ap Cuhelin26岁，母亲Gwrangen Comer23岁。他于大约1163年与Annes verch Seisyll of Coed Rhath在英国威尔斯结婚。他们至少是6个儿子的父母。他去世于1195年之后。

nameMeanings.name: Gwilym
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: equivalent of William , in use since the Middle Ages.

variants.name: Gwilim
meanings: equivalent of William , in use since the Middle Ages.

variants.name: Gwillym
meanings: equivalent of William , in use since the Middle Ages.

variants.name: William
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

nameMeanings.name: Ap
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of any of the names beginning with this syllable.

variants.name: Alexander
meanings: From the Latin form of the Greek name Alexandros, from alexein ‘to defend’ + anēr ‘man, warrior’ (genitive andros). The name became extremely popular in the post-classical period, and was borne by several individuals in the New Testament and some early Christian saints. Its use as a common given name throughout Europe, however, derives largely from the fame of Alexander the Great , King of Macedon ( 356–323 bc ), around whom a large body of popular legend grew up in late antiquity, much of which came to be embodied in the medieval ‘Alexander romances’.

variants.name: Alan
meanings: Of Celtic origin and uncertain derivation (possibly a diminutive of a word meaning ‘rock’). It was introduced into England by Breton followers of William the Conqueror , most notably Alan , Earl of Brittany, who was rewarded for his services with vast estates in the newly conquered kingdom. In Britain the variants Allan and Allen are considerably less frequent, and generally represent transferred uses of surname forms, whereas in America all three forms of the name are approximately equally common. See also Alun .

variants.name: Albert
meanings: From an Old French name, Albert, of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from adal ‘noble’ + berht ‘bright, famous’. This was adopted by the Normans and introduced by them to England, displacing the Old English form Æþelbeorht. The name is popular in a variety of forms in Western Europe, and has been traditional in a number of European princely families. It was out of favour in England for centuries, however, and the revival of its popularity in the 19th century was largely in honour of Queen Victoria's consort, Prince Albert of Saxe-Coburg-Gotha .

en.lifeSummary: When Prince Gwilym Ap Gwrwared was born about 1140, in Pembrokeshire, Wales, United Kingdom, his father, Gwnvared ap Cuhelin, was 26 and his mother, Gwrangen Comer, was 23. He married Annes verch Seisyll of Coed Rhath about 1163, in Wales, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 6 sons. He died after 1195.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Prince Gwilym Ap Gwrwared est né environ 1140, à Pembrokeshire, Pays de Galles, Royaume-Uni, son père, Gwnvared ap Cuhelin, avait 26 ans et sa mère, Gwrangen Comer, avait 23 ans. Il a épousé Annes verch Seisyll of Coed Rhath environ 1163, à Pays de Galles, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 6 fils. Il est décédé après 1195.

de.lifeSummary: Als Prince Gwilym Ap Gwrwared um 1140 in Pembrokeshire, Wales, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war sein Vater Gwnvared ap Cuhelin 26 und seine Mutter Gwrangen Comer 23. Er hat um 1163 in Wales, United Kingdom Annes verch Seisyll of Coed Rhath geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 6 Söhne. Er starb nach 1195.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Prince Gwilym Ap Gwrwared è nato il circa 1140 nel Pembrokeshire, Galles, Regno Unito, il suo padre, Gwnvared ap Cuhelin, aveva 26 anni e il suo madre, Gwrangen Comer, aveva 23 anni. Lui sposato Annes verch Seisyll of Coed Rhath circa 1163, nel Galles, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 6 figli. Morì dopo il 1195.

ja.lifeSummary: Prince Gwilym Ap Gwrwaredが約1140年にイギリスウェールズペンブロケシュアイアで生まれた時，父Gwnvared ap Cuhelinは26歳，母Gwrangen Comerは23歳であった。約1163年にイギリスウェールズでAnnes verch Seisyll of Coed Rhathと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも6人の息子の親であった。彼は1195年以後に死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Prince Gwilym Ap Gwrwared(이)가 대략 1140년 영국 웨일스 p브로케실르에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Gwnvared ap Cuhelin(은)는 26세였고, 그의 어머니 Gwrangen Comer(은)는 23세였다. 그는 대략 1163년 영국 웨일스에서 Annes verch Seisyll of Coed Rhath(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 여섯 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1195년 이후 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Prince Gwilym Ap Gwrwared nasceu aproximadamente 1140, em Pembrokeshire, Gales, United Kingdom, seu pai, Gwnvared ap Cuhelin, tinha 26 anos e sua mãe, Gwrangen Comer, tinha 23 anos. Ele casou-se com Annes verch Seisyll of Coed Rhath aproximadamente 1163, em Gales, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 6 filhos. Ele faleceu após 1195.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Prince Gwilym Ap Gwrwared родился около 1140 г. в местности пемброкешир, Уэльс, Великобритания, его отцу по имени Gwnvared ap Cuhelin было 26 лет, а матери, которую звали Gwrangen Comer, 23. Он женился на женщине по имени Annes verch Seisyll of Coed Rhath около 1163 г. в местности Уэльс, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 6 сыновей. Он умер после 1195 г.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Prince Gwilym Ap Gwrwared nació aproximadamente 1140, en Pembrokeshire, Gales, Reino Unido, su papá, Gwnvared ap Cuhelin, tenía 26 años y su mamá, Gwrangen Comer, tenía 23 años. Se casó con Annes verch Seisyll of Coed Rhath aproximadamente 1163, en Gales, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 6 hijos. Murió después de 1195.
